# openCenter Customer App Example

> Reference customer application deployment for openCenter platform using Kustomize overlays and FluxCD GitOps. Designed for multi-team environments where each team manages its own GitOps configuration in a shared FluxCD control plane.

## Operator Guidance

- Use Kustomize to compose shared gateway resources and application manifests
- Application teams manage their own repos; platform teams configure Flux GitRepository and Kustomization manifests
- Run `kustomize build .` locally before pushing changes (add `--enable-helm` for Helm-based apps)
- Validate manifests with `kubeconform <(kustomize build .)` before committing
- Use pre-commit hooks to enforce YAML linting standards
- Never commit raw secrets; reference secrets provisioned by platform team or use External Secrets Operator
- Force reconciliation after merging with `flux reconcile kustomization customer-app --with-source`
- Coordinate shared gateway modifications with other teams to avoid conflicting host rules

## Installation

### Prerequisites

Install required tooling for local development and validation.

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Install pre-commit framework for YAML linting:

```bash
# Using pip
pip install pre-commit

# Or using Homebrew
brew install pre-commit

# Register the hook in your repository
pre-commit install
```

### Clone Repository

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Application teams clone or fork the example repository to start their deployment:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/<org>/openCenter-customer-app-example
cd openCenter-customer-app-example
```

## Quickstart

### Application Team Workflow

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Quick steps for application teams to deploy their workload using the existing FluxCD control plane.

```bash
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/<org>/openCenter-customer-app-example

# 2. Modify manifests as needed (e.g., update gateway listeners)
# Edit gateway-resources/gateway.yaml, app1/deployment.yaml, etc.

# 3. Validate locally before committing
kustomize build .

# 4. Run pre-commit checks
pre-commit run --all-files

# 5. Commit and push changes
git add .
git commit -m "Update application configuration"
git push origin main
```

The platform team will provide a deploy key to add to your repository settings for Flux authentication.

### Platform Team Workflow

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Platform teams configure Flux to reconcile application team repositories.

```bash
# 1. Create Flux Git secret for repository access
flux create secret git opencenter-customer-app-example \
  --ssh-key-algorithm=ed25519 \
  --url=ssh://git@github.com/rackerlabs/openCenter-customer-app-example.git \
  -n flux-system

# 2. Distribute the public key to application team as deploy key

# 3. Create GitRepository and Kustomization manifests in cluster config repo
# (See Configuration section for manifest examples)

# 4. Commit and push to trigger Flux reconciliation
git add clusters/<cluster>/customer-managed/<app>/
git commit -m "Add customer application"
git push origin main
```

## Core Concepts

### Repository Structure

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

The repository is organized into modular components for shared resources and individual applications.

- `kustomization.yaml` — Root composition assembling gateway resources and apps
- `gateway-resources/` — Gateway API definitions reused by all apps
- `app1/` — Baseline workload with raw Kubernetes manifests
- `app2/` — Optional Helm-based workload with HelmRelease

### Multi-Team GitOps Model

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Application teams maintain their own repositories with deployment manifests. Platform teams configure Flux to watch these repositories and reconcile changes automatically. This separation enables teams to work independently while sharing infrastructure like gateways and ingress controllers.

### Gateway API Integration

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/gateway-resources/gateway.yaml

Applications use Gateway API resources (Gateway, HTTPRoute) for ingress traffic management. The Gateway resource defines listeners with TLS termination, while HTTPRoute resources in each app namespace route traffic to services.

## Common Recipes

### Deploy Basic Application (app1)

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app1/

Deploy a simple nginx-based application with Gateway API routing and cert-manager TLS.

Root kustomization.yaml:

```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
resources:
  - "gateway-resources/"
  - "app1/"
  - "app2/"
```

Deployment manifest (app1/deployment.yaml):

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web-app
  labels: { app: web }
  namespace: app1
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels: { app: web }
  template:
    metadata:
      labels: { app: web }
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 80
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "100m"
              memory: "128Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "256Mi"
```

Service manifest (app1/service.yaml):

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: web-app
  namespace: app1
  labels: { app: web }
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector: { app: web }
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
```

### Configure Gateway with TLS

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/gateway-resources/gateway.yaml

Define a Gateway resource with HTTPS listeners and cert-manager integration for automatic certificate provisioning.

```yaml
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: customer-managed-gateway
  namespace: app1
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: sample-ca-issuer
spec:
  gatewayClassName: eg
  listeners:
    - name: web-https
      port: 443
      protocol: HTTPS
      hostname: app1.demo-customer.dev.dfw3.k8s.opencenter.cloud
      allowedRoutes:
        namespaces:
          from: All
      tls:
        mode: Terminate
        certificateRefs:
          - group: ""
            kind: Secret
            name: app1-tls
```

### Route Traffic with HTTPRoute

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app1/httproute.yaml

Create an HTTPRoute to connect the Gateway listener to your application service.

```yaml
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: HTTPRoute
metadata:
  name: web-app
  namespace: app1
spec:
  hostnames:
    - "app1.demo-customer.dev.dfw3.k8s.opencenter.cloud"
  parentRefs:
    - group: gateway.networking.k8s.io
      kind: Gateway
      name: customer-managed-gateway
      namespace: app1
      sectionName: web-https
  rules:
    - backendRefs:
        - group: ""
          kind: Service
          name: web-app
          port: 80
          weight: 1
      matches:
        - path:
            type: PathPrefix
            value: /
```

### Deploy Helm-Based Application (app2)

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app2/

Deploy applications using Flux HelmRelease with values stored in Kubernetes secrets.

HelmRepository source (app2/source.yaml):

```yaml
apiVersion: source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1
kind: HelmRepository
metadata:
  name: headlamp
  namespace: headlamp
spec:
  url: https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/headlamp/
  interval: 1h
```

HelmRelease manifest (app2/helmrelease.yaml):

```yaml
apiVersion: helm.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v2
kind: HelmRelease
metadata:
  name: headlamp
  namespace: headlamp
spec:
  releaseName: headlamp
  interval: 5m
  timeout: 10m
  driftDetection:
    mode: enabled
  install:
    remediation:
      retries: 3
      remediateLastFailure: true
  upgrade:
    remediation:
      retries: 0
      remediateLastFailure: false
  targetNamespace: headlamp
  chart:
    spec:
      chart: headlamp
      version: 0.35.0
      sourceRef:
        kind: HelmRepository
        name: headlamp
        namespace: headlamp
  valuesFrom:
    - kind: Secret
      name: headlamp-values-base
      valuesKey: hardened.yaml
    - kind: Secret
      name: headlamp-values-override
      valuesKey: override.yaml
      optional: true
```

### Configure cert-manager Issuer

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app1/sample-ca-issuer.yaml

Set up a ClusterIssuer for automatic TLS certificate provisioning via cert-manager.

```yaml
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: ClusterIssuer
metadata:
  name: sample-ca-issuer
spec:
  ca:
    secretName: sample-root-secret
```

## Configuration

### Platform Team: GitRepository Manifest

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Create a GitRepository resource in your cluster configuration repository to point Flux at the application team's repo.

```yaml
apiVersion: source.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1
kind: GitRepository
metadata:
  name: customer-app
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  interval: 1m
  url: https://github.com/<org>/openCenter-customer-app-example
  ref:
    branch: main
```

Save as `clusters/<cluster>/customer-managed/<app>/customer-app-source.yaml`.

### Platform Team: Kustomization Manifest

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Create a Kustomization resource to reconcile the application repository manifests.

```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1
kind: Kustomization
metadata:
  name: customer-app
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  interval: 10m
  prune: true
  wait: true
  path: ./
  sourceRef:
    kind: GitRepository
    name: customer-app
```

Save in the same directory as the GitRepository manifest, then commit and push to trigger reconciliation.

### Pre-commit Configuration

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/.pre-commit-config.yaml

Configure pre-commit hooks to enforce YAML linting before commits.

```yaml
repos:
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/mirrors-yamllint
    rev: v1.35.1
    hooks:
      - id: yamllint
        args: ["--strict"]
```

### YAML Linting Rules

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/.yamllint

Define yamllint rules for consistent YAML formatting across the repository.

```yaml
extends: default
rules:
  indentation:
    spaces: 2
    indent-sequences: consistent
  line-length:
    max: 120
    allow-non-breakable-words: true
  truthy:
    allowed-values: ['true', 'false', 'on', 'off', 'yes', 'no']
```

## API Patterns

### Namespace Organization

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app1/namespace.yaml

Each application should define its own namespace for resource isolation.

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: app1
```

### Cross-Namespace Gateway References

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app1/httproute.yaml, openCenter-customer-app-example/app2/httproute.yaml

HTTPRoute resources can reference Gateways in different namespaces using parentRefs with explicit namespace specification.

```yaml
spec:
  parentRefs:
    - group: gateway.networking.k8s.io
      kind: Gateway
      name: customer-managed-gateway
      namespace: app1
      sectionName: web-https
```

The Gateway must allow cross-namespace routes:

```yaml
spec:
  listeners:
    - name: web-https
      allowedRoutes:
        namespaces:
          from: All
```

### Helm Values from Secrets

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app2/helmrelease.yaml

HelmRelease can load values from Kubernetes secrets for sensitive configuration management.

```yaml
spec:
  valuesFrom:
    - kind: Secret
      name: headlamp-values-base
      valuesKey: hardened.yaml
    - kind: Secret
      name: headlamp-values-override
      valuesKey: override.yaml
      optional: true
```

## Troubleshooting

### Validate Manifests Locally

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Use kustomize and kubeconform to catch errors before pushing to Git.

```bash
# Build and preview manifests
kustomize build .

# Build with Helm support (if app2 is enabled)
kustomize build . --enable-helm

# Validate against Kubernetes schemas
kubeconform <(kustomize build .)
```

### Check Flux Reconciliation Status

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Monitor Flux controller logs and reconciliation status when deployments fail.

```bash
# View kustomize-controller logs
kubectl logs -n flux-system deploy/kustomize-controller -f

# Force reconciliation after changes
flux reconcile kustomization customer-app --with-source

# Preview changes before committing
flux diff kustomization customer-app --path=./
```

### Debug Gateway Configuration

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Coordinate with other teams when modifying shared gateway resources to avoid conflicts.

- Check for conflicting hostname rules across HTTPRoute resources
- Verify Gateway listener sectionName matches in HTTPRoute parentRefs
- Ensure cert-manager annotations reference valid ClusterIssuer resources
- Confirm TLS secret names match between Gateway and cert-manager

### Pre-commit Hook Failures

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Run pre-commit checks manually to identify and fix YAML formatting issues.

```bash
# Run all pre-commit hooks on all files
pre-commit run --all-files

# Run yamllint directly with strict mode
yamllint --strict .
```

Common issues include indentation errors, line length violations, and truthy value formatting.

## Security Notes

### Secret Management

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Never commit raw secrets to Git repositories. Use platform-provided secret management solutions.

- Reference secrets provisioned by the platform team
- Integrate with External Secrets Operator for external secret stores
- Use Sealed Secrets for encrypted secrets in Git
- Update manifest references (`spec.secretName`, `valuesFrom`) to match cluster secrets

### Deploy Key Authentication

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Flux authenticates to application repositories using SSH deploy keys with restricted permissions.

```bash
# Platform team creates Flux Git secret
flux create secret git opencenter-customer-app-example \
  --ssh-key-algorithm=ed25519 \
  --url=ssh://git@github.com/rackerlabs/openCenter-customer-app-example.git \
  -n flux-system
```

The generated public key should be added as a read-only deploy key in the application repository settings.

### TLS Certificate Management

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/gateway-resources/gateway.yaml, openCenter-customer-app-example/app1/sample-ca-issuer.yaml

Use cert-manager with ClusterIssuer resources for automatic TLS certificate provisioning and rotation.

- Gateway annotations trigger cert-manager certificate creation
- Certificates are stored as Kubernetes secrets
- ClusterIssuer references CA secrets for signing
- Automatic renewal prevents certificate expiration

## Examples

### Complete app1 Structure

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/app1/

A complete example of a basic application with namespace, deployment, service, HTTPRoute, and cert-manager configuration.

Directory structure:
```
app1/
├── deployment.yaml          # Nginx deployment with resource limits
├── httproute.yaml          # Gateway API routing configuration
├── kustomization.yaml      # Kustomize resource list
├── namespace.yaml          # app1 namespace definition
├── sample-ca-issuer.yaml   # cert-manager ClusterIssuer
├── sample-selfsigned-ca.yaml
├── sample-selfsigned-issuer.yaml
└── service.yaml            # ClusterIP service
```

Kustomization (app1/kustomization.yaml):
```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
resources:
  - namespace.yaml
  - deployment.yaml
  - service.yaml
  - httproute.yaml
  - sample-ca-issuer.yaml
```

### Multi-Application Gateway

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/gateway-resources/gateway.yaml

A single Gateway resource can serve multiple applications with different hostnames and TLS certificates.

```yaml
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: customer-managed-gateway
  namespace: app1
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: sample-ca-issuer
spec:
  gatewayClassName: eg
  listeners:
    - name: web-https
      port: 443
      protocol: HTTPS
      hostname: app1.demo-customer.dev.dfw3.k8s.opencenter.cloud
      allowedRoutes:
        namespaces:
          from: All
      tls:
        mode: Terminate
        certificateRefs:
          - group: ""
            kind: Secret
            name: app1-tls
    - name: headlamp-https
      port: 443
      protocol: HTTPS
      hostname: headlamp.demo-customer.dev.dfw3.k8s.opencenter.cloud
      allowedRoutes:
        namespaces:
          from: All
      tls:
        mode: Terminate
        certificateRefs:
          - group: ""
            kind: Secret
            name: headlamp-tls
```

Each application creates an HTTPRoute referencing the appropriate listener by sectionName.

### Team Ownership Labeling

Source: openCenter-customer-app-example/README.md

Track Kustomization ownership across teams using labels for better operational visibility.

```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1
kind: Kustomization
metadata:
  name: customer-app
  namespace: flux-system
  labels:
    toolkit.fluxcd.io/team: acme
spec:
  interval: 10m
  prune: true
  wait: true
  path: ./
  sourceRef:
    kind: GitRepository
    name: customer-app
```

Query resources by team:
```bash
kubectl get kustomization -n flux-system -l toolkit.fluxcd.io/team=acme
```
